mechanism_narrative: Persistently near-zero FEDFUNDS reduces the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, sustaining elevated equity valuations. As long as FEDFUNDS remains below 0.5% with no upward trend, forward S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days are biased positive relative to historical average.
regime_hint: zero lower bound / accommodative monetary policy
failure_condition: If 60-day forward returns during periods of FEDFUNDS < 0.5% are not statistically greater than the full-sample mean (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE falls below a threshold consistent with near full employment (e.g., approaching pre-crisis lows), markets begin pricing in earlier Fed tightening, compressing forward equity returns over the next 20 trading days as discount-rate expectations rise.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE falls below 6.0% while FEDFUNDS remains near zero
regime_hint: late-cycle labor market tightening ahead of rate normalization
failure_condition: If SPX 20-day forward returns following UNRATE crossing below 6.0% are positive in more than 60% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, the hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) reflects broad-based consumer and residential investment demand strength that jointly forecasts positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days; neither series alone is sufficient because retail momentum without housing follow-through has historically been a weaker signal.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month annualized growth > 4% AND HOUST 3-month annualized growth > 5%
failure_condition: If joint-trigger instances produce forward 20-day returns statistically indistinguishable from unconditional baseline (p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month increases in INDPRO signal expanding industrial capacity utilization and corporate output, which feed through to improving earnings expectations and support positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: If the correlation between trailing INDPRO 3-month growth and forward 60-day SPX returns falls below 0.03 Rank IC over any 3-year rolling window, hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: In a zero-lower-bound regime where FEDFUNDS has been pinned near zero for an extended period, equity returns are primarily driven by liquidity and yield-seeking flows rather than growth fundamentals, so forward 20-day S&P 500 returns are positively biased whenever FEDFUNDS remains below 0.25% for 12+ consecutive months.
regime_hint: extended zero lower bound liquidity regime
failure_condition: If forward 20-day returns during qualifying zero-lower-bound windows are not significantly greater than non-qualifying windows (p > 0.05), the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp acceleration in nonfarm payroll growth (PAYEMS) above a threshold signals stronger-than-expected labor demand, prompting a short-term positive repricing of growth expectations that lifts S&P 500 returns over the following 5 trading days.
failure_condition: If SPX 5-day forward returns following PAYEMS surprises above threshold are not significantly positive (p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) combined with low and stable inflation (CPIAUCSL) reflects a non-inflationary growth regime that supports sustained equity appreciation; the interaction matters because strong GDP growth accompanied by rising inflation historically triggers valuation compression via tightening expectations, while GDP growth with stable inflation does not.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 QoQ annualized growth > 2% AND CPIAUCSL YoY < 2.5%
failure_condition: If joint-trigger periods show forward 60-day SPX returns not statistically different from baseline (p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in CPIAUCSL year-over-year inflation above a threshold level triggers market expectations of earlier monetary tightening, compressing forward S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days as discount rates are expected to rise.
failure_condition: If SPX 20-day forward returns following CPI threshold breaches are positive in more than 60% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, the hypothesis is rejected.
